sections.paragraph:
λαχμός, οῦ, ὁ (s. λαγχάνω) an object used as a device for making a decision through sortilege, lot (schol. on Soph., Aj. 1281; schol. on Eur., Hippol. 1057; schol. on Theocr. 8, 30; Etym. Mag. p. 519, 10; Eustath.) λαχμὸν βάλλειν ἐπί τινι cast lots for someth. GPt 4:12 (cp. Just., D. 97, 3).—DELG s.v. λαγχάνω.

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

Theocr Theocr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

Eustath Eustath , XII a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 586
